title: SQL Transform
description: Runs user-supplied SQL inside an embedded DuckDB engine.

sql.description: The SQL to execute inside the embedded DuckDB engine.
sql.x-datuplet-doc: Reference input tables by their logical names (e.g. FROM orders) and produce outputs with CREATE TABLE <output-logical-name> AS SELECT .... No bucket templating or storage paths — the Data Gateway mediates all reads and writes, so the SQL stays environment-agnostic. This component is credentials-clean: no S3, GCS, or Lakekeeper credentials are ever passed to it.

threads.x-datuplet-doc: Each thread keeps its own per-pipeline hash-aggregate state and decode buffers, so raising this multiplies both in-memory state and concurrent spill-writer pressure. The default of 2 balances using a typical 1-2 vCPU pod allotment against per-thread arena cost. Bump to 4+ only when the host has dedicated CPU and memory headroom and wall-clock matters more than peak memory.

memory.x-datuplet-doc: DuckDB spills to temp_directory when an operator exceeds this. It is NOT a hard RSS cap — parquet decode buffers, page cache, and go-duckdb FFI state bypass it. The default of 1GB is deliberately conservative for shared single-node clusters where even a brief overshoot can trigger kubelet eviction. Raise it per-pipeline when a workload genuinely needs more in-memory hash-table room than spill can absorb (e.g. a JOIN of two large tables).

max_temp_size.description: Hard cap on DuckDB spill-to-disk usage; the query errors when it is reached.
max_temp_size.x-datuplet-doc: This is the real guardrail against node-killing spill. The kubelet's ephemeral-storage limit is soft (sampled roughly every 10s and can be outrun by a fast spiller), whereas DuckDB's internal cap is synchronous. Set it below the pod's ephemeral-storage limit so DuckDB fails the query before the kubelet evicts the pod, and well below the node's free boot disk so a runaway spill cannot crater the node.

temp_directory.description: Directory DuckDB spills large aggregates to.
temp_directory.x-datuplet-doc: The component creates this directory at start if it does not exist. It should live on the pod's ephemeral storage; DuckDB's max_temp_size caps how much it may fill here.
